flavorNotes: Tropical fruit, mint, floral, caramel — wild, persistent mint throughout
coffeeDescription: Coffeein (Slovakia) — Colombia Las Flores. Washed anaerobic, Chiroso variety. SCA 88pts. Flavor: tropical fruit, caramel, floral, mint. Roasted 24.02.2026. Frozen 15.03.2026, thawed 07.04.2026.
notes: Home pour-over, Sculptor 064S. Grind 14.5, 20g/300ml, 91C, bloom 40g/45s, 4-phase ~3:40. KEY: phases 2 and 3 at 3-4g/s (slower than usual) — critical for bringing out persistent mint character. Grind 14 = mint dominant throughout but unbalanced. Grind 16 = more balanced but mint fades after first sip. Grind 14.5 + slow middle pours = sweet spot: tropical fruit opening, caramel body, strong persistent mint throughout entire cup and in aftertaste. Wildest and most expressive pour-over yet — each sip tells a story. Extraction time stays ~4 min regardless of grind (14–16) due to coffee density — controlled by pour rate instead. Frozen 15.3.2026 (19 days after roast), thawed 7.4.2026.

flavorNotes: Citrus, mulberry, plums, ripe nectarines — mild, elegant, fruity tea character
coffeeDescription: Café Jordán — Etiopie Suke Quto. Natural process, light roast. Flavor: citrus, mulberry, plums, ripe nectarines. 86pts. Roasted 11.03.2026. Frozen 15.03.2026, thawed 07.04.2026.
notes: Dialed on two kits. OFFICE (Baratza Encore ESP): grind 20, 20g/300ml, 90C, bloom 40g/45s, 4-phase, ~3:30. HOME (Sculptor 064S): grind 15, same recipe, ~3:20. Sculptor 15 confirms filter zone for light Ethiopian naturals — consistent with Chelbesa. Mild, elegant natural Ethiopian — fruity tea character throughout, light body, no aftertaste, no bitterness. Not a wow coffee but the fruity tea note appears consistently every cup including cold, which elevates it. Pleasant daily work coffee. Frozen 15.3.2026 (only 4 days after roast), thawed 7.4.2026 — effectively very fresh.

flavorNotes: Flat, generic, dark roast americano character — nothing interesting
coffeeDescription: Kilikafee Exports · Roastery · Shop — Kilimanjaro Royal Coffee. 100% Arabica AA/AB/PB grade, grown 1,200–2,200m on slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro. Medium roast, medium grind. Pre-ground. Processed & distributed by D&J Distributors Co. Ltd, Moshi, Tanzania. Roasted 19.11.2025.
notes: V60, home pour-over. Pre-ground medium — cannot adjust grind. 20g/300ml, 94C, bloom 40g/45s, single main pour, done 2:45. Completely flat, burned, generic dark roast character — no origin flavors, no complexity. Souvenir coffee from Tanzania, mass-market product. Undrinkable. Remainder used as plant fertilizer. Abandoned after one brew.

flavorNotes: Plums, lemon sorbet, green tea
coffeeDescription: Kmen Coffee Roasters — Ethiopia Iron Lion. Named after the Bob Marley song Iron Lion Zion. Natural process, espresso roast. Flavor: plums, lemon sorbet, green tea. 250g. Roasted 10.12.2025.
notes: Home espresso, Sculptor 064S. Dialed over 2 days. Started at 4.0 (choked), worked up to 6.5 (too fast), sweet spot at 5.9/40g. Final: grind 5.9, 18g/40g (1:2.22), 28s, 92°C. Tea and citrus opening, plum body, clean finish. 40g yield key — 36g too bitter, 41.6g too flat. My Pick.

flavorNotes: Jasmine, bergamot, hint of sweetness — clean, analytical, layered
coffeeDescription: Kmen Coffee Roasters — Ethiopia Chelbesa, washed filter. 250g. Roasted 22.01.2026. Second bag, home dial-in.
notes: V60, home filter (Sculptor 064S). Second bag. Grind 15 — significantly coarser than previous zone, consistent with grinder maturation shift. 20g/300ml, 91°C, ~3:30. Jasmine and bergamot, clean and analytical. Less expressive than office Encore version. One-dimensional once dialed. Would not repurchase.

flavorNotes: Jasmine, white grapes, bergamot, hidden sweet-bitterness
coffeeDescription: Kmen Coffee Roasters — Kmenova spolecnost, roasted in Ceske Budejovice. Ethiopia Chelbesa — washed process. Flavor: green jasmine tea, white grapes, bergamot. 250g filter. Roasted 22.01.2026.
notes: V60, office filter setup (Baratza Encore ESP). Dialed in 3 brews. Grind 20 on Encore, 20g/300ml, 91C, bloom 40g/45-50s, four pours targeting 3:30-3:40. Best of both worlds — stronger body with jasmine mainly upfront, fruit notes more hidden and integrated, hidden sweet-bitterness underneath. Clean, balanced, no aftertaste. Started at grind 21 (3:15, cleaner but thinner), found sweet spot at 20 (3:35, better body and edge).

flavorNotes: Cherry, berries — heavy body, bitter, no sweetness or acidity
coffeeDescription: Hippo Café (DKJ Tran Company s.r.o., Praha) — Family Café Roaster. Catimor Vietnam — Cầu Đất, Đà Lạt. Variety: Catimor. Natural. Flavor: cherry, berries. 250g. Roasted 25.02.2026.
notes: Home espresso, Sculptor 064S. Grind 3.8, 18g/36g, 34s, 90°C. Heavy, bitter, no sweetness or acidity. Acceptable in milk only. Traditional Vietnamese dark roast style — not suited for modern specialty espresso. Archived.

flavorNotes: Jasmine tea, complex, adjustable
notes: V60, home filter setup (Timemore Sculptor 064S). Grind 10.2, 20g/320ml, 91C, 4-phase pour, 3:25. Jasmine tea character, complex and adjustable. Excellent.

flavorNotes: Caramel, panela, sweet stone fruit, very stable
coffeeDescription: Mia Coffee — Costa Rica Cerro San Luis. Washed process.
notes: Office espresso, Eureka Zenith 65E. Grind 2.0, 18g/36g, 28s, 92C. Caramel, panela, sweet stone fruit. Very stable extraction.

flavorNotes: Apple, chocolate, caramel
coffeeDescription: Chroast is a specialty roastery from Hradec Kralove, Czech Republic, founded 2018. Kafe protoze koks je drahej — 50% Colombia + 50% Brazil regional blend, mixed processing (Natural + Washed). Light roast (195-202C). Flavor: apple, chocolate, caramel. 1kg bags. Roasted 26.01.2026.
notes: Office anchor coffee. Weird good — normal chocolate-forward, easy to dial, very stable and forgiving. Flat but pleasant, nothing wild. Perfect daily driver for office — colleagues enjoy it, no drama. Grind 1.75 on Eureka, 20-24s shots.

flavorNotes: Rhubarb, blackcurrant
coffeeDescription: Pikolo — specialty roastery from Šumperk, Jeseniky mountains. Kenya Kainamui AA — washed process, grade AA, Kirinyaga region. Varietal: SL28, SL34. Flavor: rhubarb, blackcurrant. Roasted 5.1.2026.
notes: V60 filter, home setup (Timemore Sculptor 064S). 18g/300ml (1:16.7), 90C, bloom 45g/40-45s. Best at 90C vs 92C — 92C compressed acidity into blunt rhubarb, 90C gave sharper cleaner red currant forward. High clarity, bright structured acidity, very responsive to temperature changes.

coffeeDescription: Gift bag from Emirates Airlines and Praha Letiste (Prague Airport) — Christmas 2025 promotional coffee. Unknown roastery and origin. Commercial blend, not specialty.
notes: Airport gift. Likely similar decoration-tier to Idee Kaffee. May try as French press filler eventually.

coffeeName: Costa Rica San Ramon de Altura
flavorNotes: Floral perfume, figs, honey, white tea
coffeeDescription: DOT Coffee — Czech specialty roastery. Costa Rica San Ramon de Altura — farm San Ramon Cafe de Altura, Tarrazú, 1400m, Caturra and Catuai. Flavor: floral perfume, figs, honey, white tea. 250g. Roasted 21.01.2026.
notes: V60, home filter. Classic elegant Costa Rican filter — Earl Grey / white tea character, silky body, low-medium sweetness, tea-like acidity, short clean finish. Also tried Honey Mode at 94-95C for more sweetness and fig notes. Second Costa Rica that really impressed.

flavorNotes: Acidic, chocolatey — difficult to dial, better in milk
coffeeDescription: Playground Coffee — Hamburg, St. Pauli. Moonwalker Espresso — bestselling all-rounder. Brazil (Cerrado Mineiro: Mundo Novo, Red Catuai, Bourbon, Icatu, Catucai) + Guatemala (Huehuetenango: Caturra, Typica). Natural + Washed. 100% Arabica. Roasted January 2026.
notes: Acidic, difficult to dial. Better in milk drinks. Balanced crowd-pleaser style conflicts with preference for expressive fruit-forward profiles. Espresso only intended use.

flavorNotes: Floral, elegant acidity, light body, sweetness arrives late
notes: Occasional / exploratory. Interesting but not a daily driver. Floral, elegant acidity, light body. Lower temp (88C) and tighter ratio (1:1.78). Sweetness arrives late.

flavorNotes: Ripe banana, blueberry, strawberry — strong, fluid, expressive
coffeeDescription: Tarbon — Czech specialty roastery, Dvur Kralove nad Labem. A.I — selected Vietnamese arabica, experimental anaerobic/fermented processing. Christmas limited edition. Notes of ripe banana, blueberry, strawberry. 100% Arabica, 227g.
notes: Benchmark first impression — first bag (4★ experience): first espresso that truly wowed. Took many shots to dial in, once there: distinct banana at start of shot, tropical fruit throughout, fluid mouthfeel. Worth the effort. Second bag: entirely different roast — undialable, unstable, not comparable. First bag 4★, second bag 1★ at best. Overall 2★ averaged.

flavorNotes: Chocolate, nuts, dried fruit
coffeeDescription: Coffeespot — Moravian specialty roastery, Babice, Czech Republic. 12th annual limited Christmas blend — Brazil Diamond Santos + Honduras San Andres SHG. 100% Arabica, 250g. Roasted 13.11.2025.

flavorNotes: Complex, comforting, sweet, works well with milk
coffeeDescription: Upraženo — Beskydy mountains specialty roastery. Beskydská směs Zimní edice — limited winter seasonal blend. Complex, comforting, sweet.
notes: Seasonal winter blend. Complex for a blend, comforting sweetness, excellent in milk drinks. Would buy the next winter edition.

flavorNotes: Low acidity, calm, mild chocolate — flat when pushed
notes: Support / milk coffee role. Low acidity, calm, mild chocolate — loses character when pushed. Better suited for milk drinks only.

flavorNotes: Balanced, mild fruit, subtle sweetness, coherent
coffeeDescription: BONZO — local espresso blend, Brazil-led. Reliable anchor role.
notes: Reliable daily espresso. Easy to dial, coherent profile. Not expressive enough to rebuy but does the job.

flavorNotes: Caramel, chocolate, nuts
coffeeDescription: Master Vietnam Espresso — Vietnamese/Brazilian blend, roasted in Czech Republic. 70% Arabica / 30% Robusta. Dark roast. Flavor: caramel, chocolate, nuts. 250g. Second bag in freezer.
notes: Early period — unseasoned grinder, dial-in parameters not reliably recorded. Second bag in freezer — worth retrying with seasoned setup for comparison.
